D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Metal vs Dielectric Reflectance

toggle metal or glass and watch one stay bright at every angle while the other dips at Brewster

Metals have a large extinction coefficient k, so light cannot enter — only reflect. Dielectrics have k ≈ 0 and obey the real-Fresnel zero at Brewster's angle.

Toggle the surface. Glass and water show the textbook Brewster dip in R_p (crimson). Aluminum and silver stay near 90 % reflective at every angle — the reason mirrors are made of metal.
